USD.catalyst: The U.S. Fed 3.75%. Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh, speaking from the ECB Forum in Sintra, Portugal, on July 1, declined to provide specific guidance on the upcoming July rate decision, contributing to the current softening of the greenback. While geopolitical risks in the Strait of Hormuz provide a floor for the currency, a supply rebound in the oil market and OPEC+ plans for an August output hike are weighing on energy-linked inflation expectations. The USD CESI remains robust at +282, highlighting a fundamental gap against G10 peers despite the recent cooling in hawkish policy bets.

sources.title: Dollar near two-week lows as rate-hike bets recede, embattled yen in focus - CNBC
US futures rise and Asian shares trade mixed as oil prices decline with increased output - The Sun Chronicle
Global shares mixed as US futures rise; oil slips after OPEC+ plans August output hike - The Sun Chronicle
US futures and Asian shares are mixed while oil prices decline as some exporters opt to raise output - AP News

GBP.catalyst: Sterling is facing a critical technical test near its 200-day SMA as market participants weigh a disappointing S&P Global Construction PMI of 38.4 for June against hawkish rhetoric from Bank of England policymaker Catherine Mann. Governor Andrew Bailey, participating in the Sintra policy panel on July 1, remains in focus as banks suggest the BoE could tweak leverage rules this week to boost demand for Gilts and lower public borrowing costs. Despite the weak construction data, the FTSE 100 notched a weekly gain on July 3, supported by financials and miners, which has provided a stabilizing backdrop for the pound against the euro.

sources.title: Bank of England could boost bond demand with leverage rule tweak, banks say - Reuters
London's FTSE indexes post weekly gain as financials, miners advance - KITCO
Fed Chairman Kevin Warsh declines to hint at July rate decision - CNBC

EUR.catalyst: ECB 2.25%. While the currency is structurally capped by a +150bp Fed-ECB carry disadvantage, recent sentiment is supported by a significant improvement in the July Sentix survey and a surge in German factory orders. ECB policymakers Joachim Nagel and Moulin have signaled a "hawkish hold" stance, though the EUR CESI at -11 continues to lag the U.S. fundamental profile. Traders are also monitoring the ECB’s development of a retail digital euro, which diverges from the U.S. private-sector-led stablecoin strategy.

sources.title: Yen Hits Fresh 40-Year Low Versus Dollar; Traders Alert for Possible FX Intervention - marketscreener.com
CNBC Daily Open: ECB goes 'back to basics' - CNBC
Europe And China Diverge From The U.S. On The Future Of Money - Forbes

JPY.catalyst: The Japanese yen remains pinned near a 40-year low of 161.81 against the dollar, a level not seen since 1986, keeping markets on high alert for Ministry of Finance intervention. Goldman Sachs has revised its USD/JPY forecasts upward to 162 in the three-month horizon, citing the persistent +275bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage and an energy "supply bust" that favors the dollar. BoJ 1.0%. Speculative positioning remains heavily short, with COT data showing leveraged funds net short over 115,000 contracts.

sources.title: Yen to extend historic slump, while AI and energy 'supply bust' to support the U.S. dollar: Goldman - CNBC
Yen Hits Fresh 40-Year Low Versus Dollar; Traders Alert for Possible FX Intervention - marketscreener.com
Asian shares follow Wall Street higher, while the Japanese yen hits a 39-year low against the dollar - AP News
JPY/USD Yen Hits Four-Decade Low in Historic Slide That’s Rattled Japan - Bloomberg.com

AUD.catalyst: The Australian dollar is currently characterized by a downside bias within a broader neutral band, pressured by cooler domestic inflation and weak employment data. Corporate activity provided a localized catalyst on July 6 as Genesis Minerals launched a 5.6 billion AUD bid for gold producer Vault Minerals, highlighting ongoing consolidation in the mining sector. Despite this M&A flow, the currency is drifting away from recent highs as commodity price volatility and a lack of hawkish RBA catalysts limit upside momentum. Analysts at UOB suggest further consolidation is likely as the currency struggles to find a clear directional driver in the absence of fresh central bank guidance.

sources.title: Mining company Genesis bids 5.6 billion Australian dollars to acquire gold miner Vault - Bitget

CAD.catalyst: The Canadian dollar is trading near 1.4210 against the USD, pressured by uncertainty surrounding the U.S.-Mexico-Canada Agreement (USMCA) renewal deadline. While the loonie hit a 10-day high on July 2 following soft U.S. jobs data and positive domestic manufacturing growth, it remains on track for a steep monthly decline as analysts cut rate hike forecasts for the Bank of Canada. Governor Tiff Macklem’s appearance at the Sintra forum on July 1 underscored the cautious outlook, with the RBC Business Outlook Survey now the primary focus for gauging confidence amid energy price shocks. Retail sentiment is extremely stretched, with 93% of traders short USD/CAD, creating a high risk of a short-squeeze higher.

sources.title: Analysts cut Canadian dollar forecasts as USMCA uncertainty clips rate hike chances - KITCO
Canadian dollar hits 10-day high as US job growth slows - KITCO
Canadian dollar heads for steep monthly decline ahead of USMCA deadline - KITCO

CHF.catalyst: The Swiss franc is underperforming as the U.S. dollar captures safe-haven flows, with USD/CHF aiming for the 0.8065 area amid broad greenback strength. Technical indicators, including a bearish MACD hint on the daily charts, suggest a potential deeper unwind of recent franc strength as the currency loses its "safe-haven" edge to higher-yielding alternatives. The pair remains sensitive to the widening interest rate differential between the Swiss National Bank and the Federal Reserve, with the franc increasingly used as a funding currency in the current high-volatility environment.

NZD.catalyst: The New Zealand dollar is facing acute volatility ahead of the July 8 Reserve Bank of New Zealand (RBNZ) policy meeting, with ANZ and shadow board polls signaling a "line-ball" decision on a potential hike to 2.50%. NZD/USD is trading near 0.5688, burdened by a -0.83% real rate—the most accommodative in the G10—and a significant speculative fade as COT data shows leveraged funds net short 23,314 contracts. While Westpac expects the RBNZ to hold the official cash rate at 2.25% due to declining oil prices, BNY analysts suggest the central bank is set for further tightening to combat persistent domestic pressures.

sources.title: Westpac: Reserve Bank of New Zealand has time to wait for more data - Bitget

NOK.catalyst: The Norwegian krone remains range-bound with a slight softening bias as it tracks the decline in global oil prices following OPEC+ announcements regarding August output increases. In the absence of specific domestic data surprises or Norges Bank official commentary on July 6, the currency is largely functioning as a high-beta play on European energy sentiment. Market participants are looking toward upcoming inflation data to determine if the central bank will maintain its hawkish stance relative to its G10 peers.

SEK.catalyst: The Swedish krona is currently trading in a neutral range against the euro and dollar, lacking fresh domestic catalysts to break recent technical levels. The currency remains sensitive to broader Eurozone investor sentiment, which saw an improvement in the July Sentix survey, but it has yet to see a significant idiosyncratic bid. Traders are monitoring Riksbank communications for any shift in policy tone following recent producer price data from the broader Eurozone, though the SEK remains largely sidelined pending new fiscal or monetary developments.
